key_insight: Trajectory-aware flow matching injects BESO optimisation history into a conditional generative model so topology designs can be sampled quickly while preserving mechanical feasibility.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid candidate generation for topology optimisation under changing loads, volume fractions, and design settings without costly iterative sensitivity-based updates or inference-time guidance.
method: Start from a Gaussian field and learn a flow to a reference topology using linear FMTO.
Replace the simple endpoint path with volume-fraction-indexed BESO trajectories to define the target velocity field.
Use moderate trajectory weighting to balance physical guidance and transport flexibility during training.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests showing better compliance-related performance, volume-fraction satisfaction, topology fidelity, and far fewer sampling steps than a diffusion baseline, with the best overall results under limited data at moderate trajectory weight.
watch_item: Too much trajectory guidance can over-constrain the learned transport and hurt stability or diversity, so trajectory weight and anchor density need careful tuning.
paper_title: Trajectory-Aware Flow Matching for Topology Optimisation
